lede: A look at February 11 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Paul Bocuse and Thomas Edison, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Saratov Airlines Flight 703
events.desc: Saratov Airlines Flight 703 crashes near Moscow, Russia with 71 deaths and no survivors.

events.title: North Korea
events.desc: North Korea test fires a ballistic missile across the Sea of Japan.

events.title: Murder of Özgecan Aslan
events.desc: A university student is murdered as she resists an attempted rape in Turkey, sparking nationwide protests and public outcry against harassment and violence against women.

events.title: Pluto
events.desc: Pluto crosses Neptune's orbit, ending a nearly 20-year period when it was closer to the Sun than the gas giant; Pluto is not expected to interact with Neptune's orbit again until 2231.

events.title: Nelson Mandela
events.desc: Nelson Mandela is released from Victor Verster Prison outside Cape Town, South Africa after 27 years as a political prisoner.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Second day of the Battle of Bukit Timah is fought in Singapore.

events.title: Friedrich Ebert
events.desc: Friedrich Ebert (SPD) is elected President of Germany.

events.title: University College London
events.desc: University College London is founded as University of London.

events.title: Carnival tragedy of 1823
events.desc: Carnival tragedy of 1823: About 110 boys are killed during a human crush at the Convent of the Minori Osservanti in Valletta, Malta.

events.title: Pedro Sarmiento de Gamboa
events.desc: A naval expedition led by Pedro Sarmiento de Gamboa founds Nombre de Jesús, the first of two short-lived Spanish settlements in the Strait of Magellan.

births.name: Thomas Edison
births.line: American engineer and businessman, developed the light bulb and phonograph

deaths.text: Alexander Griboyedov, Russian poet, playwright, and composer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blaise Eastern Orthodox liturgics
Christian feast day: Cædmon, first recorded Christian poet in England, c. 680 CE (Anglicanism)
Christian feast day: Peter of Jesus Maldonado
